name: Cordes Law, PLLC
description_short: Charlotte-based law firm specializing in bankruptcy representation for debtors and creditors, plus family law and debtor-creditor services.
description_long:
Cordes Law, PLLC is a Charlotte, North Carolina law firm founded by Stacy C. Cordes and staffed by experienced women attorneys. The firm was established to provide legal services focused on the Greater Charlotte area, with founding partners who are native to the region. The firm operates from 1057 E. Morehead Street, Suite 120, in Charlotte and serves clients through phone (704-332-3565) and email (info@cordes-law.com).

The firm offers three primary practice areas: bankruptcy law, family law, and debtor-creditor representation. In bankruptcy, Cordes Law represents individual debtors in Chapter 7 filings and represents creditors in Chapters 7, 11, and 13 cases. Their family law practice covers divorce, custody, child support, alimony, equitable distribution, prenuptial agreements, adoption, and mediation. The debtor-creditor practice includes debt settlement negotiations, loan modifications, foreclosure representation, and creditor rights advocacy.

Cordes Law distinguishes itself through a stated commitment to personalized, individualized service and listening to each client's specific situation. Client testimonials highlight the firm's responsiveness, empathy, and willingness to prioritize client outcomes over compensation. One client noted that Attorney Cordes assisted with a post-bankruptcy issue that another firm had abandoned, resolving it within a week. Multiple reviews emphasize the firm's ability to make complex legal matters understandable and their professionalism balanced with a down-to-earth approach.

The firm is best suited for individual debtors filing Chapter 7 bankruptcy, creditors seeking representation in bankruptcy proceedings, and families requiring comprehensive legal support. However, as a specialized legal practice, they are not a consumer finance product and cannot provide immediate financial relief, credit repair, or debt consolidation services themselves—they provide legal representation and guidance.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Represents both debtors and creditors across multiple bankruptcy chapters (7, 11, 13)
Founded and staffed by experienced women attorneys with deep roots in greater Charlotte area
Offers comprehensive family law services including divorce, custody, child support, and mediation
Client testimonials highlight responsiveness and willingness to work on cases other firms abandoned
Debtor-creditor practice includes debt settlement negotiations and loan modification assistance
Provides initial consultation to assess specific client situations
All three practice areas integrate well for clients facing concurrent bankruptcy and family law issues
cons: Limited geographic service area (Charlotte and greater Charlotte region only)
No indication of flat fees or transparent pricing structure on website
Does not provide credit repair, debt consolidation, or direct financial relief services
No mention of evening or weekend consultation hours for working clients
Cannot immediately resolve financial emergencies; legal process timelines vary
best_for: Charlotte-area individuals filing Chapter 7 bankruptcy seeking personalized legal representation
Creditors and lenders needing representation in bankruptcy cases across multiple chapters
Families navigating concurrent bankruptcy and family law issues (divorce, custody, support)
Individuals seeking debt settlement negotiations or loan modification guidance
diagnosis: Cordes Law is best for Charlotte-area individuals or creditors needing bankruptcy legal representation, particularly those also managing family law matters. Critical caveat: this is a legal services firm, not a consumer finance product—it provides guidance and representation through bankruptcy proceedings but cannot provide immediate debt relief, credit repair, or financial consolidation services.
services: Chapter 7 bankruptcy filings for individual debtors
Creditor representation and rights in Chapters 7, 11, and 13 bankruptcy cases
Child custody and child support determination
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
